blurb:
Somewhere out there is a person who's been waiting for a ginger brindle Boxer-Corgi mix who will fetch a ball until your arm gives out and then entertain himself when you need a break. That person should go meet Asher.

He's about three years old and 40 pounds, which sounds manageable until you realize he's, in the listing's own words, a lot of dog in a medium-sized package. He went through the prison training program, learned a ton, and is currently in a foster home doing great. His foster parents have figured out he's surprisingly good at keeping himself busy indoors, so he'd apparently make a solid office dog. He also loves walks and will ask for several of them per day, so if you like getting outside, you two are going to get along fine.

A few things worth knowing: he's guarded toys and beds from other dogs before, so he needs to be your only pet. No cats either. Kids 15 and up are a good fit since younger kids and toddlers make him uncomfortable. He can be barky when he spots other dogs on walks, but the Mostly Mutts trainer will walk you through exactly how to handle it. He'd also really thrive with a fenced yard, and an apartment probably isn't the right fit.

Asher is in a foster home now through Mostly Mutts in Kennesaw. Visit mostlymutts.org to start the process or set up a meet.
shelter_address: 1462 Kennesaw Due West Rd NW, Kennesaw, GA 30152
scoring_notes:
• Narrow adoption fit — only pet, no cats, kids 15+, fenced yard required — limits urgency and reduces newsletter conversion potential
• Ginger brindle Boxer-Corgi mix is a genuinely unusual combo that could spark curiosity, but the restrictions will temper reader excitement
• No strong indicators of extended shelter time and a fairly standard rescue arc make him a lower-priority feature compared to peers with more compelling stories

blurb:
Josie has floppy ears, a nubby little tail, and enough energy to keep up with just about anyone willing to try. She's a hound mix, about six years old and 35 pounds, and she's been through the adoption process once already. The family that took her home wasn't quite ready for her pace, so she came back to Mostly Mutts. That's not a knock on Josie. That's just a mismatch.

Her foster rates her energy at a 7 or 8 out of 10, but here's the thing: her cuddle level matches it. She wants to run around and play hard, and then she wants to be right next to you on the couch. She's affectionate, playful, and a total people dog. She does some mouthy play, gentle nibbling during games, nothing aggressive, just how she expresses herself when she's having a good time.

She needs to be your only dog and no cats. For kids, her foster thinks 10 and up is a good match given her energy and play style. She's a Mostly Mutts training program grad and has learned a lot since coming into rescue.

If you like being active and want a dog who's going to be genuinely excited to do everything with you, Josie is worth a visit. She's in a foster home right now through Mostly Mutts in Kennesaw. Go to mostlymutts.org to submit an inquiry and set up a meeting.
shelter_address: 1462 Kennesaw Due West Rd NW, Kennesaw, GA 30152
scoring_notes:
• Returned due to a family mismatch — not her fault — is a classic underdog angle that motivates readers who want to be the one who gets it right
• High-energy plus high-cuddle combination with training program credentials makes her a compelling, well-rounded feature for active-lifestyle readers
• One prior return and active foster placement suggest she has been circling the system long enough to deserve a real spotlight push

blurb:
Bolton came into rescue underweight with thin fur and a past nobody knows much about. Whatever he went through before, you'd never guess it now. He's nine years old, about 56 pounds, and his foster family says he lights up every room he walks into. That's a hard thing to fake.

He's a hound-boxer mix, so he's got two modes: nose to the ground, sniffing every inch of the yard with total focus, and flopped on the couch next to you being completely ridiculous. His foster calls him sweet and a little goofy, which tracks. He loves playing tag with other dogs, running at them and then bolting away hoping they'll chase him. He's also great with kids around 8 to 12, so if you've got some running around the house, he's likely to fit right in.

No cats, and toddlers would probably get bowled over by accident. But if you have an older dog who likes to play, bring them in for a meet and greet. Bolton's been friendly with the dogs he's met in foster care and at the shelter.

He went through the prison training program and has learned a lot. At nine, he's past the chaos of puppyhood and into the best part, loyal, calm enough to hang out, still playful when you want him to be. Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Head to mostlymutts.org or stop by to meet him.
shelter_address: 1462 Kennesaw Due West Rd NW, Kennesaw, GA 30152
scoring_notes:
• Senior dog with an unknown rough past who now lights up every room is a deeply compelling redemption story with broad reader appeal
• Prison training program graduate with a hound-boxer goofiness that practically writes the newsletter headline itself
• Arrived underweight with thin fur and limited history — clear indicators of a longer, harder road that makes his wait feel urgent and worth championing

shelter_name: Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue & Adoption, Inc.
blurb:
Gaia was out on her own when a good neighbor took her in so she could have a safe place to have her kittens. Mostly Mutts helped find homes for all of them. Now it is her turn. She is about 2 years old, around 8.5 pounds, and fully done with the mom chapter. She is ready to just be someone's cat.

She is not a lap cat, and she will be the first to tell you that. What she is instead is a cat who will come find you when she wants pets, chat with you about her day (she is reportedly quite vocal), and then go do her own thing when she is satisfied. She has already figured out the exact schedule in her foster home and holds everyone accountable to it, especially when Greenies and her bedtime Churu are involved. She is the kind of cat who makes a house feel like it has a personality.

She does best in a quieter home with older kids or adults who are fine with a cat that sets the terms of affection. She has been easy-going with other cats in her foster home, so a second cat is on the table if the match is right.

Gaia is with Mostly Mutts in Kennesaw. Call or email to set up a meet, and bring your patience for the snack schedule.
scoring_notes:
• Stray-mom-done-with-motherhood narrative is relatable and warmly human, making her instantly likable to readers
• Strong personality details — snack schedule, bedtime Churu, daily chattiness — give the newsletter a fun, vivid character to feature
• Moderate shelter timeline with no strong urgency signals, but her independent charm broadens her adoptability appeal significantly

shelter_name: Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue & Adoption, Inc.
blurb:
Gina has had a rougher road than most kittens her age, and she is still one of the sweetest cats at Mostly Mutts. She came in with her siblings as part of the Go-Gos litter, got adopted, and then got returned when she started having some stomach issues. Months of vet visits and food trials later, she is doing better, though she does still vomit occasionally and no one has pinned down exactly why. Her foster calls her the best cat ever. That is a direct quote.

She is about 11 months old and around 5 pounds, and she is probably going to stay that way. If you have always wanted a cat that keeps that kitten energy and kitten size well into adulthood, Gina is genuinely the answer to that. She is playful, she loves to snuggle, and she is independent enough to entertain herself when you are busy.

Because she eats a special diet, the ideal setup is a home where she is the only pet, or one where separate feeding is easy to manage. She has done well with the adult cats in her foster home and was not bothered by a leashed dog, so a calm, cat-friendly household could work too. Gentle kids are welcome.

Gina is at Mostly Mutts in Kennesaw. Reach out by phone or email to start the process, and ask about her specifically so the team can walk you through her care routine before you visit.
scoring_notes:
• Adopted-and-returned arc with ongoing mystery health issue creates real reader empathy and urgency
• Foster's "best cat ever" endorsement plus perpetual kitten size are irresistible newsletter hooks
• Months of vet visits and a return adoption signal an extended shelter journey that raises urgency to find her the right home

shelter_name: Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue & Adoption, Inc.
blurb:
Iceman was adopted from Mostly Mutts as a kitten, spent five years with his family, and ended up back when their situation changed. None of that is on him. He is 11 pounds, fully grown, and exactly the kind of cat who is ready to settle in and be somebody's companion without any drama.

He likes hanging out near his people and watching the world go by. He is litter trained, up to date on vaccines, neutered, microchipped, and combo tested negative. He is basically move-in ready. His foster home is still getting to know him, so more personality details are coming, but his previous family had him for five years and clearly he did just fine.

One thing to know: he was not a fan of other male cats in his previous home. A female cat roommate could work well, and he loved the kids in a foster home a few years back, so a family with gentle older kids is probably a good fit. Dogs are unknown, but a calm, cat-friendly dog would be worth a meet and greet.

Iceman is with Mostly Mutts in Kennesaw. His adoption fee is $175. Call or email to ask about him and set up a visit.
scoring_notes:
• Five years in a loving home before an owner-circumstance return is a heartstring-pulling story that readers will rally behind
• Move-in-ready status — neutered, vaccinated, microchipped, combo-tested — removes friction and makes him a strong practical adoption candidate
• A previous multi-year adoption followed by a return, plus a foster home still getting to know him, signals meaningful time in the system and genuine need for spotlight
